title: cordia trachyphylla
intro: Cordia trachyphylla, also known as the Wild Pear or Cordia tree, is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Bignoniaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities, which contribute to its various health benefits. The extracts and parts of the Cordia trachyphylla plant are used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and powders, to treat conditions like fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cordia trachyphylla include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine.
